description: 58M post-MI on simvastatin. Clinical ASCVD triggers secondary prevention. Currently on moderate-intensity statin; should upgrade to high-intensity.

actions.label: Switch to high-intensity statin therapy (atorvastatin 40-80 mg or rosuvastatin 20-40 mg)
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA COR I, LOE A: patients with clinical ASCVD aged ≤75 should receive high-intensity statin with goal of ≥50% LDL-C reduction. Patient is on simvastatin (moderate-intensity) post-MI. LDL 115 mg/dL is above the ≥70 mg/dL threshold where therapy intensification is recommended. Switch to atorvastatin 40-80 mg or rosuvastatin 20-40 mg.

actions.label: Recheck LDL-C in 4-12 weeks after statin intensification
actions.rationale: After switching to high-intensity statin, monitor LDL-C to assess whether ≥50% reduction achieved and whether LDL-C falls below 70 mg/dL. If not at goal, consider adding ezetimibe.

actions.label: Optimize blood pressure management
actions.rationale: BP 134/82 on lisinopril is borderline elevated post-MI. ACC/AHA BP guideline recommends target <130/80 for patients with ASCVD. May need dose adjustment or additional agent.

contraindications.label: Continue moderate-intensity statin without intensification
contraindications.rationale: Patient has clinical ASCVD (post-MI). ACC/AHA COR I recommendation is high-intensity statin, not moderate-intensity. Continuing simvastatin alone is suboptimal.

contraindications.label: Discontinue statin therapy
contraindications.rationale: Secondary prevention with statin is among the strongest evidence-based recommendations in cardiology. Discontinuation is contraindicated.
